source_jobs.title: Lead Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PLURAL:

The assets that produce, store, and consume electrons will define the next era of the economy. But today, financing these assets is slow, expensive, and fragmented—locked behind legacy intermediaries and limited access.



Plural is passionately building the infrastructure to change that. We tokenize everything from solar projects to batteries to bitcoin mines, using smart contracts and programmable workflows to reduce friction and open access to capital.

On Plural, the nodes of a distributed grid are financed by a network of institutional, individual, and DeFi investors. This approach lowers costs, increases transparency, and enables scalable capital formation for the energy transition—trusted, composable, and built for efficiency.




THE OPPORTUNITY:

Plural is seeking an experienced Product / UI Engineer to help craft the core user experience of our platform. You will play a pivotal role in defining how investors and asset operators interact with next-generation energy infrastructure.



 - Build and maintain our existing Next.js Typescript React codebase to push out new features and slicken the UX.

 - Provide best-in-class experiences for both Institutional and retail users across TradFi and DeFi.

 - Develop robust front-end systems that are maintainable, scalable, and aligned with long-term product architecture.

 - DApp development—build and maintain UI components that connect to EVM-based smart contracts.

 - Stay current with modern front-end technologies and emerging patterns in fintech, marketplace products, and web3.




QUALIFICATIONS:

Required

 - 4+ years of professional experience with TypeScript and React.

 - 2+ years of experience developing TypeScript-based dApps.

 - UX experience with a strong eye for intuitive user-centric design.

 - Power user of AI development tools like cursor and demonstrated ability to create MVPs quickly

 - Hungry, driven self-starter with a demonstrated track record of high agency, attention to detail, and thrives in ambiguity.

Bonus:

 - Experience in fintech, exchanges, or DeFi companies

 - Production experience with Next.js.

 - Exposure to or interest in EVM-based smart contracts and web3 development




WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Competitive salary and equity options

 - Comprehensive health insurance

 - Close mentorship from experienced product and technical leaders.

 - A collaborative, mission-driven culture pushing the boundaries of finance, energy, and blockchain technology.

 - Innovative Culture—we are a supportive and collaborative team pushing the boundaries of finance with blockchain.

source_jobs.title: Infrastructure Senior Associate
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Plural is hiring an Infrastructure Senior Associate to support the tokenization of infrastructure projects across energy, data centers, transportation, public-private partnerships, and other critical infrastructure sectors. This role sits at the intersection of economic development, infrastructure, finance, and tokenization.



In addition to financial analysis, due diligence, and management of asset tokenizations, this role includes supporting the buildout of a first-of-its-kind infrastructure tokenization platform in partnership with public-sector institutions to facilitate private capital into public works projects. You'll help establish operational processes, conduct project diligence, structure financing solutions, and support pilot projects from concept through execution.



This is a highly entrepreneurial role for someone motivated by building new systems and deploying capital into real-world infrastructure.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Project Evaluation & Structuring - Analyze energy and infrastructure projects for tokenization readiness, develop financial models, and design capital structures that align public and private stakeholder interests.

 - Platform Operations Buildout - Establish and document processes for asset onboarding, investor workflows, compliance procedures, and offering preparation as part of tokenization platform development.

 - Transaction Execution - Conduct due diligence on infrastructure assets, prepare offering materials, coordinate legal and regulatory work, and support projects from structuring through close

 - Financial Analysis & Modeling - Build detailed project finance models, scenario analyses, and investment memos to support decision-making and capital deployment

 - Stakeholder Coordination - Serve as liaison across partners including issuers, investors, public agencies, counsel, technical teams, vendors, politicians, beauracrats (mention this typo in your application to prove your attention to detail), and more.

 - Program Management - Track implementation milestones, manage workstreams, and ensure deliverables are met across legal, technical, and capital formation tracks





WHO YOU ARE

 - Experience - 3–6+ years in infrastructure finance, project finance, investment banking, consulting (infrastructure/energy/public sector focus), or development finance. Early-stage startup experience is a plus

 - Financial Skills - Strong financial modeling capabilities with experience analyzing infrastructure projects, understanding credit structures, and evaluating long-term cash flow assets

 - Builder Mentality - Comfortable operating in ambiguity, creating processes from scratch, and taking ownership of open-ended problems without clear playbooks

 - Communication - Able to translate complex financial, technical, and policy considerations into clear, actionable plans for diverse stakeholders including public agencies, legal teams, and capital providers

 - Mission-Driven - Genuinely motivated by infrastructure development, economic development, and using innovative financing tools to solve real-world capital access problems

 - Execution-Oriented - Balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution that gets stuff done!


WHAT WE THINK A GREAT FIT COULD LOOK LIKE

 - Former infrastructure or project finance banker ready to trade deal volume for depth and impact — you want to build something new rather than execute the next mandate

 - Management consultant with finance experience ready to move from advisory to execution— you're tired of making recommendations and want to see capital actually deploy

 - Development finance professional seeking to build innovative platforms — you understand blended capital and public-private partnerships but want to apply those skills to frontier solutions

 - Startup operator with finance background looking for mission-driven work — you've experienced the 0-to-1 buildout process and want to apply that to real-world infrastructure

 - Project fina

source_jobs.title: Originations Lead
source_jobs.description:
Location: SF Office

Employment Type: Full Time

Location Type: In-Person (Hybrid)

Department: Assets and Investments

Compensation: Estimated Base Salary $150K – $195K • Offers Equity



About the Role

Plural is hiring an Originations Lead to run the sourcing of new renewable energy companies onto the Plural platform. This role sits at the intersection of clean energy finance, capital markets, and early-stage company building.

You will own the top of the origination funnel — identifying and building relationships with renewable energy developers and project sponsors who need to raise capital, then working closely with Plural's brokerage team to bring those opportunities to life.

What You'll Do

 - Origination & Pipeline Development – Proactively identify and pursue renewable energy developers, project sponsors, and asset owners who need to raise equity, tax equity, preferred equity, or senior debt.

 - Asset & Portfolio Sourcing – Identify and pursue renewable energy assets and portfolios that are candidates for tokenization on the Plural platform, independent of an active capital raise.

 - Relationship Management – Develop and maintain deep relationships with CFOs, VPs of Finance, and Heads of Capital Markets at renewable energy companies. Be the person they call when they are thinking about their next raise.

 - Market Coverage – Represent Plural at clean energy industry events, conferences, and trade associations (RE+, ACP, SEIA, etc.), building deal flow and brand recognition simultaneously.

 - Initial Qualification & Diligence – Conduct first-pass evaluation of inbound and sourced opportunities before the structuring team goes deep.

 - Product Feedback & Development – Serve as the voice of the developer in product conversations. Gather requirements from prospective and existing clients, work directly with the product team to help shape the roadmap.

 - Market Intelligence – Feed insights back to the product and brokerage teams on what developers are asking for, what terms they need, and where the market is moving.
   
   

Who You Are

 - Experience – 6–10 years in clean energy finance, project finance, investment banking, capital markets, or a closely adjacent field. Early-stage startup experience is a plus.

 - Network – You have warm, genuine relationships with decision-makers at renewable energy developers who trust your judgment and take your calls. This is the most important thing.

 - Entrepreneurial Mentality – You enjoy building relationships from scratch, following up persistently, and converting interest into commitment.

 - Domain Fluency – You understand how renewable energy deals are structured — tax equity, ITC transfers, preferred equity, senior secured debt — well enough to have a credible first conversation.

 - Builder Orientation – Comfortable operating with minimal process and high ambiguity. You will write the originations playbook, not follow one.

 - Communication – Able to represent Plural credibly with sophisticated counterparties and translate complex financing concepts clearly.

What we think a great fit could look like

 - A renewable energy developer or IPP finance professional ready to work on the other side of the table

 - A clean energy investment banker or advisor who has spent years originating or managing client relationships at a firm

 - A BD or coverage professional from a capital markets fintech — you've spent time at a Fintech platform or Tax Credit Marketplace building developer relationships

 - A tax equity or structured finance professional who has deep developer relationships and wants to bring those relationships to a platform built for the next era of clean energy finance

 - A project finance professional seeking a more entrepreneurial path — you've built the models and supported the deals, now you want to own the relationships and shape the pipeline yourself.

Location & Travel

 - Must be San Francisco based. Flexibility for hybrid.

 - Periodic domestic travel r

notes: Small sample size, but Plural appears to be a fintech company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles.
